note: dashboard_server.py only. No changes to market_stream.py, state_hub.py, or any other file.

context.why: Polymarket WebSocket only sends delta price_change events. After reconnects on pre-game or low-activity markets, no price updates arrive for tracked tokens. The mark goes stale (stale_after_sec=15.0) and stays stale indefinitely. No REST fallback exists. This causes 'Market feed stale' on the dashboard for all pre-game positions.
context.root_cause: market_stream.py _on_open re-subscribes on reconnect, but Polymarket does not push the current price on subscription — only on actual price changes. ARI/NYM NO token saw no price movement after 2 reconnects → mark_age=238s.
context.fix_summary: Add a polling fallback in _stream_positions_mark() that fires a Polymarket CLOB REST call for any stale mark older than 30s, throttled to once per 20s per slug, injecting the result into state_hub via GLOBAL_STATE_HUB.update_mark(). This is entirely in dashboard_server.py — no stream changes.

step1_module_level_additions.where: After the AUTO_STOP_LOSS_PCT line (~line 56), before the Logging section
add: STALE_REST_POLL_SEC = 30.0     # Only trigger REST poll if mark is older than this
STALE_REST_THROTTLE_SEC = 20.0 # Min seconds between REST polls for same slug
_rest_poll_ts: dict[str, float] = {}  # slug → last REST poll wall-clock time

step2_helper_function.where: Add as a new function immediately before _stream_positions_mark() (~line 389)
code: def _poll_stale_mark(slug: str, market_id: str, asset_id: str) -> None:
"""Fetch current price from Polymarket CLOB REST API and inject into state_hub."""
best_bid = float(bids[0]['price']) if bids else None
best_ask = float(asks[0]['price']) if asks else None
current_price = best_bid if best_bid is not None else best_ask
spread = round(best_ask - best_bid, 6) if (best_bid is not None and best_ask is not None) else None
log.info('[STALE_POLL] %s → cp=%.4f bid=%s ask=%s', slug, current_price, best_bid, best_ask)
log.warning('[STALE_POLL] %s → empty book (no bid or ask)', slug)

step3_stream_positions_mark_change.where: In _stream_positions_mark(), immediately after line 398: marks = GLOBAL_STATE_HUB.snapshot(stale_after_sec=15.0) and before line 399: trades = _fetch_trades(limit=100)
insert: # REST fallback: for any mark stale >30s that hasn't been polled in the last 20s,
# fetch current price from Polymarket CLOB REST and inject into state_hub.
marks = GLOBAL_STATE_HUB.snapshot(stale_after_sec=15.0)  # re-snapshot with fresh REST data
step3_stream_positions_mark_change.note: The lock is held only for the fast timestamp check+claim. HTTP calls happen outside the lock so concurrent SSE connections do not deadlock. Re-snapshot only runs if at least one poll fired.

steps: Confirm dashboard_server.py is the only file changed (git diff --name-only or equivalent)
Confirm CLOB_BOOK, STALE_REST_POLL_SEC, STALE_REST_THROTTLE_SEC, _rest_poll_ts, _rest_poll_lock are added at module level
Confirm the REST fallback block is inserted between the snapshot() call and the trades = _fetch_trades() call
Trigger verification: kill the WebSocket connection (or wait for stale mark), then call http://localhost:8900/api/stream/state and confirm stale=False within ~35s
Confirm [STALE_POLL] log lines appear in dashboard_server output
Confirm mark_source='rest_fallback' in SSE positions payload after a REST poll fires
Confirm throttle: second SSE tick does NOT re-poll (log shows no second [STALE_POLL] within 20s)

do_not: Edit any file other than dashboard_server.py
Restart any process (only code change — operator will restart after review)
Add REST polling inside a background thread (keep it inline in the SSE handler)
